title: Sour Crunchy Algerian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A flavorful and crunchy solo-dinner dish featuring sour chicken wings with a touch of ferment to enhance its savory taste, suitable for Jain-friendly meals.

ingredients.name: fermented vegetables (e.g. pickles or brine)
ingredients.notes: use fresh or pre-made ferment based on availability and preference

ingredients.name: sour sauce (e.g. tamarind or fermented bean paste)

instructions: Preheat your wok over high heat. Add sesame oil and wait until hot.
Add chicken wings to the wok, season with salt, and stir-fry for about 5 minutes on each side or until golden brown.
Remove chicken from the wok and set aside.
In the same wok, add fermented vegetables (if using fresh, finely chop them), ginger, garlic, and red pepper flakes. Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
Return chicken to the wok with its juices, pour in sour sauce, and stir well until everything is coated evenly.
Turn off heat and serve hot.
